ai_search_trends.title: AI-generated videos to maximally drive a target brain region
Building a real-time AI tutor for 5-year-olds
Ben Bernanke Joins Anthropic Oversight Trust
OpenAI faked inability to search training data, hid billions of logs, NYT says
How do you use Vim in the era of AI?
China may restrict foreign access to Chinese open-source AI models
Ask HN: Do we need a support group for developers alienated by LLMs?

anthropic_claude_news.title: The Download: Claude’s inner workings and OpenAI’s “super app”
anthropic_claude_news.summary: MIT Technology Review's Download leads with Anthropic's interpretability breakthrough: researchers found a hidden internal space where Claude puzzles over concepts before answering — the clearest view yet inside a frontier model's reasoning. Automation angle: interpretability wins build enterprise trust; cite this when clients ask whether agent behavior can be audited.

anthropic_claude_news.title: OpenAI unveils long-awaited 'super app' as rivalry with Anthropic intensifies
anthropic_claude_news.summary: OpenAI unveiled ChatGPT Work, a GPT-5.6-powered agent workspace for professionals that combines chat, coding, and document workflows — a head-on rival to Anthropic's Claude Cowork. The Claude-vs-ChatGPT battle has moved from chatbots to full agentic workspaces. Automation angle: master one agent workspace deeply and demo cross-app workflows — that's the skill both ecosystems are now hiring around.

anthropic_claude_news.title: Bun ditches Zig for Rust with help from Claude Fable 5, writes over a million lines of code in 11 days
anthropic_claude_news.summary: The Bun JavaScript runtime was rewritten from Zig to Rust with Claude Fable 5 doing most of the work — over a million lines of code in 11 days. It is one of the largest publicized examples of an AI model executing a full-codebase migration. Automation angle: million-line agent migrations are the new proof-of-capability bar — record your own (smaller) migration as portfolio evidence.

anthropic_claude_news.title: Former Fed chair Ben Bernanke joins Anthropic's AI oversight trust
anthropic_claude_news.summary: Former Fed chair and Nobel laureate Ben Bernanke joined Anthropic's AI oversight trust, adding macroeconomic heavyweight governance as the company scales. Automation angle: governance hires signal enterprise-sales maturity — expect more regulated-industry Claude deployments worth building for.

ai_business_automation.title: How Deutsche Telekom is rewiring telecommunications with AI
ai_business_automation.summary: OpenAI detailed how Deutsche Telekom is rebuilding itself as an AI-native telco: customer service, employee workflows, network operations, and voice interfaces are all being rewired around AI agents. A rare end-to-end look at AI automation across one large enterprise. Automation angle: telco-style service workflows (triage, ticketing, voice) are exactly what freelance automation builders can replicate for SMBs.

ai_business_automation.title: Employees adopting AI faster than organisations, says McKinsey survey
ai_business_automation.summary: A McKinsey survey finds employees are adopting AI faster than their organisations: AI tops technology spending priorities, yet most companies remain stuck at early deployment while staff self-serve with consumer tools. Automation angle: that adoption gap IS the AI-automation job market — companies need people who turn shadow AI use into governed workflows.

ai_business_automation.title: The 'learn to code' era is over - and employers are on the hook for reskilling now
ai_business_automation.summary: ZDNet argues the 'learn to code' era is over and employers now own reskilling: AI has shifted demand from writing code to directing and validating it, echoing the last decade's tech-jobs drives. Automation angle: position yourself as the reskilling answer — teach teams to work with agents, not compete with them.

ai_business_automation.title: Special delivery: Italy's postman joins the AI infrastructure race
ai_business_automation.summary: Italy's Poste Italiane is betting its €13.5 billion transformation on AI infrastructure, turning a 12,600-branch postal network into a distributed AI services operation spanning pensions, logistics, and citizen services. Automation angle: legacy institutions buying AI capacity will outsource workflow design — a services opportunity, not just a headline.

ai_business_automation.title: How to shrink the token budget without shrinking the team
ai_business_automation.summary: Nvidia's Jensen Huang says every engineer now comes with a token budget attached, and this AI News piece maps concrete tactics — caching, routing, smaller models for mechanical steps — for shrinking LLM spend without shrinking output. Automation angle: token-cost engineering is a billable skill today; productize an 'AI bill audit' for teams.

quantum_ai_research.title: Google Study Shows Quantum Computer Can Learn From Its Own Errors While It Computes
quantum_ai_research.summary: Google researchers showed a quantum computer that uses machine-learning-style feedback to learn from its own errors mid-computation, adapting error correction on the fly instead of relying on fixed decoding. It is a concrete case of AI methods making quantum hardware more reliable. Automation angle: watch ML-decoded error correction — it is the pattern that will make quantum-in-the-loop AI services practical.

quantum_ai_research.title: TSMC’s Photonic Ramp Could Be the Quiet Catalyst in AI Chips - 24/7 Wall St.
quantum_ai_research.summary: TSMC is quietly ramping photonic interconnect and co-packaged optics capacity, which analysts flag as a sleeper catalyst for AI chips. Photonic computing and optical I/O directly target the bandwidth wall that limits large-model inference. Automation angle: cheaper AI inference from optical interconnects lowers the unit cost of every agent workflow you sell.

quantum_ai_research.title: The Other Direction: How AI Could Become The Operating System For Quantum Computing - Seeking Alpha
quantum_ai_research.summary: A Seeking Alpha analysis argues the near-term win is AI acting as the control layer — calibration, compilation, and error mitigation — for quantum processors, rather than quantum speeding up AI. AI as the 'operating system' for QPUs is already showing up in vendor roadmaps. Automation angle: AI-for-quantum tooling is a niche where automation skills transfer directly — quantum labs need workflow builders too.

quantum_ai_research.title: IQM to Deliver Quantum Computer for Europe’s LUMI AI Factory - The Quantum Insider
quantum_ai_research.summary: IQM will deliver a quantum computer for Europe's LUMI AI Factory, wiring a QPU directly into a flagship AI supercomputing site for hybrid HPC + AI + quantum workloads. Automation angle: hybrid quantum-AI pipelines will need orchestration glue — the same skill set as agent workflow design.

quantum_ai_research.title: Spinout developing hardware needed for quantum computing and the next wave of AI applications - University of Cambridge
quantum_ai_research.summary: A University of Cambridge spinout is building hardware aimed at both quantum computing and next-generation AI workloads, betting the two stacks converge at the device level. Automation angle: hybrid quantum/AI hardware startups are early hirers of generalist automation engineers — track their careers pages.

quantum_ai_research.title: Quantum Computing (QUBT) Acquires NHanced And Lands NeuraWave AI Deployment Deal - Yahoo Finance
quantum_ai_research.summary: Quantum Computing Inc. acquired chip packager NHanced and landed a NeuraWave deployment deal pairing its photonic quantum hardware with AI workloads. Automation angle: quantum firms pivoting toward AI deployment deals signal where the near-term revenue actually is.

quantum_ai_research.title: MagiQware Raises €575K Pre-Seed Round to Advance AI Software For Quantum Computing - The Quantum Insider
quantum_ai_research.summary: MagiQware raised a €575K pre-seed round to build AI software for quantum computing, using machine learning to tune and operate quantum hardware. Automation angle: tiny seed rounds in AI-for-quantum mean junior-friendly teams — a wedge for a first AI job with no legacy hiring bar.

quantum_ai_research.title: Quantum Software Engineering in Practice: FPGA and AI Integration for Quantum Certification
quantum_ai_research.summary: An arXiv software-engineering paper details how FPGA control electronics and AI models are being integrated for quantum device certification, an early template for disciplined quantum + AI engineering practice. Automation angle: papers like this define the toolchain the field will hire for — skim the stack, not the math.

product_showcase_opportunities.title: AMD Developer Hackathon: ACT II
product_showcase_opportunities.summary:
To participate in the hackathon, simply click Sign up with AMD. If you are not already a member of the AMD AI Developer Program (ADP), you will need to create an account.

ADP members can access AI tutorials, experts, and community support. In addition, new members can claim a free month of DeepLearning.ai pro, $100 in AMD GPU cloud credits, and $50 in Fireworks AI API credits.

All hackathon participants will receive on hackathon launch additional compute and API credits for their projects.
Rea

ARC White-Box Estimation Challenge 2026
product_showcase_opportunities.summary: Given the weights of a neural network, can you predict its expected per-neuron activations more accurately than running it many times?

product_showcase_opportunities.title: IndiaAI Startups Global - International Acceleration Programs for Indian AI Startups
product_showcase_opportunities.benefits: Placement into international acceleration tracks, plus government backing and global market access.
product_showcase_opportunities.summary: MeitY / IndiaAI international acceleration tracks placing Indian AI startups into global programs. Rolling cohorts.

product_showcase_opportunities.title: Y Combinator (AI startups)
product_showcase_opportunities.eligibility: Any early-stage startup with a founding team; pre-launch and idea-stage are welcome.
product_showcase_opportunities.benefits: ~$500K on the standard deal, a 3-month program, and the lifelong YC network + Demo Day.

product_showcase_opportunities.title: Antler Residency (AI)
product_showcase_opportunities.eligibility: Solo founders welcome — no company or co-founder needed.
product_showcase_opportunities.benefits: Pre-seed cash, a co-founder matching residency, and hands-on mentorship.
product_showcase_opportunities.summary: Day-zero residency for founders incl. AI; rolling cohorts across 30+ cities.

product_showcase_opportunities.title: Techstars AI Accelerators
product_showcase_opportunities.eligibility: Early-stage startups; apply to the city/track that fits your team.
product_showcase_opportunities.benefits: Seed investment, a 3-month mentor-driven program, and a global investor network.
product_showcase_opportunities.summary: Multiple AI-focused Techstars programs; rolling applications by location.

product_showcase_opportunities.title: NVIDIA Inception
product_showcase_opportunities.eligibility: Any AI/ML startup at any stage — free to join.
product_showcase_opportunities.benefits: GPU and cloud credits, go-to-market support, and introductions to VCs.
product_showcase_opportunities.summary: Free program for AI/ML startups: GPU credits, go-to-market, VC intros. Rolling.

product_showcase_opportunities.title: Google for Startups AI Accelerator
product_showcase_opportunities.eligibility: Seed-to-Series-A AI startups; regional tracks including India.
product_showcase_opportunities.benefits: An equity-free program, Google mentorship, and cloud credits.
product_showcase_opportunities.summary: Equity-free AI accelerator with seasonal cohorts and regional tracks (incl. India).

product_showcase_opportunities.title: Google Selects 20 Indian AI Startups for 2026 Accelerator Program - Siliconindia
product_showcase_opportunities.summary: Google Selects 20 Indian AI Startups for 2026 Accelerator Program - Siliconindia

product_showcase_opportunities.title: Google Selects 20 AI-First Indian Startups For Accelerator India 2026 Cohort - INDIA New England News
product_showcase_opportunities.summary: Google Selects 20 AI-First Indian Startups For Accelerator India 2026 Cohort - INDIA New England News

ai_music_copyright_laws.title: RIAA, A2IM, IMPALA, and Others Announce Track-Level ‘Labeling Program’ for AI Audio, Target ‘Broad, Global Adoption’ Across DSPs
ai_music_copyright_laws.summary: The RIAA, A2IM, IMPALA and other music bodies announced a track-level labeling program for AI-generated audio, pushing for broad global adoption across streaming services. It is the industry's first coordinated disclosure standard for machine-made music. Automation angle: labeling standards create compliance tooling demand — metadata pipelines for AI audio are a startable niche.

ai_music_copyright_laws.title: Record industry proposes AI labeling system for streaming platforms
ai_music_copyright_laws.summary: Music Business Worldwide reports the record industry is formally proposing an AI labeling system to the world's biggest streaming platforms, requiring disclosure of AI involvement per track. Automation angle: if DSPs adopt this, every distributor will need automated AI-content classification — build the detector service.

elon_musk_ai_vision.title: Grok 4.5 Cuts Coding-Agent Cost 80%: Near-Frontier Speed, Higher Hallucinations - Tech Times
elon_musk_ai_vision.summary: xAI's Grok 4.5 cuts coding-agent costs by roughly 80% versus frontier rivals, with near-frontier speed but a higher hallucination rate — the trade Musk is making to win developers on price. Automation angle: test Grok 4.5 as the cheap tier in a model-routing setup, but gate it with verification steps to catch the extra hallucinations.

elon_musk_ai_vision.title: Cursor Integrates SpaceXAI’s New Grok 4.5 Model For Coding And Agentic Work - Pulse 2.0
elon_musk_ai_vision.summary: Cursor integrated Grok 4.5 for coding and agentic work — notable because xAI says the model was built alongside Cursor after their $60B partnership, making it the first frontier model co-developed with a coding-tool vendor. Automation angle: model-tool co-design is the new moat; expect Claude Code-optimized models to answer.

elon_musk_ai_vision.title: SpaceXAI launches Grok 4.5 model for coding, agentic tasks - ET BrandEquity
elon_musk_ai_vision.summary: xAI launched Grok 4.5 as its new flagship, positioned squarely at coding and agentic tasks with claimed 'Opus-class' performance at sharply lower prices. Automation angle: benchmark deltas matter less than price-per-solved-task — measure that before switching any client workload.

elon_musk_ai_vision.title: SpaceXAI’s Grok 4.5 Undercuts Anthropic and OpenAI on Coding Agent Pricing - DevOps.com
elon_musk_ai_vision.summary: DevOps.com details how Grok 4.5's pricing undercuts both Anthropic and OpenAI on coding agents, opening a price war on the agentic tier rather than the chat tier. Automation angle: falling agent prices expand what's economical to automate — re-quote projects you priced six months ago.

elon_musk_ai_vision.title: Grok Imagine - x.com
elon_musk_ai_vision.source: Elon Musk on X (via Google News)
elon_musk_ai_vision.summary: Musk promoted Grok Imagine, xAI's image/video generation feature, as it rolls out more widely on X — part of pushing Grok from chatbot to full creative suite. Automation angle: media generation inside X means distribution-native AI content — a channel worth testing for reach.

elon_musk_ai_vision.title: Musk acts fast, but can it last? - Semafor
elon_musk_ai_vision.summary: Semafor asks whether Musk's speed — shipping Grok 4.5, restructuring xAI under SpaceX, and rapid-fire AI product launches — is sustainable, or whether the pace trades away reliability. A useful read on how Musk's AI strategy actually operates. Automation angle: Musk's ship-fast pattern sets the tempo the whole AI market reacts to — plan content around his launch cycles.

elon_musk_ai_vision.title: SpaceX's near-term AI payoff seen tethered to Earth, not outer space
elon_musk_ai_vision.summary: Analysts say SpaceX's near-term AI payoff is terrestrial: data centers and AI infrastructure, not space applications, will drive returns from the xAI merger. Automation angle: follow the infrastructure spend — it predicts where AI capacity gets cheap next.

elon_musk_ai_vision.title: Elon Musk Says His Goal Is for SpaceX to Be Worth More than the Entire Earth
elon_musk_ai_vision.summary: Musk said his goal is for SpaceX — which now houses xAI and its AI ambitions — to be worth more than the entire Earth, tying his AI vision to an interplanetary economic pitch. Automation angle: rhetoric aside, xAI's capital access means sustained cheap Grok pricing — factor that into tool choices.

unaddressed_ai_problems.title: Meta AI image detector fails to identify some of its own cropped AI images: Analysis
unaddressed_ai_problems.summary: Reuters found Meta's own AI image detector failed to verify 55% of Meta-generated images after simple cropping. Provenance tooling that breaks under trivial edits leaves platforms, journalists, and courts with no reliable way to prove what is synthetic — and nobody has shipped a robust fix. Automation angle: crop-resistant provenance checking is an open product gap with paying customers (newsrooms, insurers, courts).

unaddressed_ai_problems.title: AI can't replace mental health therapists. But here's where it might make a difference
unaddressed_ai_problems.summary: AI chatbots absorb overflow demand from stretched mental-health systems, but this analysis shows nobody has solved safe escalation: bots can't reliably detect crisis, hand off to humans, or carry clinical accountability. Vulnerable users get the gap. Automation angle: the unsolved piece is the human-handoff workflow, not the model — a buildable, defensible niche.

unaddressed_ai_problems.title: Microsoft’s carbon emissions went up 25 percent last year
unaddressed_ai_problems.summary: Microsoft's carbon emissions rose 25% last year as AI datacenter buildout outpaced its climate commitments — and no vendor yet offers a credible per-workload carbon accounting or mitigation path for AI compute. Automation angle: AI carbon reporting is an unclaimed tooling category as EU disclosure rules tighten.

ai_business_opportunities.title: Tencent moves to buy majority stake in Manus after Beijing forced Meta to unwind its $2 billion deal
ai_business_opportunities.summary: Tencent is negotiating a majority stake in AI-agent startup Manus at a $2B valuation after Beijing forced Meta to unwind its acquisition. Signal: general-purpose agent products are strategic assets both superpowers want onshore. Startable idea: vertical Manus-style task agents for one industry (property management, clinics, logistics brokers) — the generalists keep getting bought before they go deep. Automation angle: pick one vertical, automate its top-3 workflows end-to-end, and you have both a product and a portfolio.

ai_business_opportunities.title: SK Hynix raises $26.5B in the biggest foreign IPO in US history, is urged to build new US fabs
ai_business_opportunities.summary: SK Hynix's record $26.5B US IPO confirms capital is flooding AI infrastructure while application-layer competition stays thin. Startable idea: infrastructure-adjacent services — GPU cost optimization audits, inference-bill dashboards, capacity brokering for SMBs — ride the spend without needing capital. Automation angle: every dollar into chips creates ten automation problems downstream; sell the shovel-sharpening.

ai_business_opportunities.title: [Weekly funding roundup July 4-10] VC inflow remains flat
ai_business_opportunities.summary: Indian startup funding stayed flat at $107M this week, but investors keep concentrating on deeptech and AI. Startable idea: in a muted funding market, revenue-first AI services (automation retainers for mid-size Indian firms) beat venture-scale plays — and India's GCC boom supplies the clients. Automation angle: flat VC weeks are the best time to sell efficiency — companies buy cost reduction when they can't raise growth capital.

global_ai_news.title: Hugging Face’s CEO on why companies are done renting their AI
global_ai_news.summary: Hugging Face CEO Clem Delangue says companies are done renting AI: open-source models are booming as enterprises move to own and fine-tune their stacks rather than pay per-token forever. Automation angle: open-model deployment skills (vLLM, LoRA, self-hosting) are rising in job listings — add one to your portfolio.

global_ai_news.title: People on X Are Getting Fooled by the Dumbest AI Slop We’ve Ever Seen
global_ai_news.summary: Futurism reports X users are being fooled en masse by crude AI-generated World Cup content, showing detection and media literacy still lag even for low-quality fakes. Automation angle: platform-grade slop detection remains unsolved — a real product gap.

global_ai_news.title: The Fed wants AI investor Marc Andreessen to help figure out if AI can tame inflation
global_ai_news.summary: The Fed has asked AI investor Marc Andreessen to help assess whether AI can tame inflation — central banks are now formally studying AI-driven productivity as a macro lever. Automation angle: macro validation of AI productivity strengthens every ROI pitch you make to clients.

global_ai_news.title: Fidji Simo steps down from OpenAI’s No. 2 role
global_ai_news.summary: OpenAI's No. 2 executive Fidji Simo is stepping down after extended medical leave, leaving a leadership gap just as the company juggles GPT-5.6, ChatGPT Work, and IPO positioning. Automation angle: exec churn at frontier labs shifts product roadmaps — watch who inherits the AGI-deployment portfolio.

global_ai_news.title: Chip giant SK Hynix raises $26.5bn as shares surge in bumper US listing
global_ai_news.summary: SK Hynix raised $26.5bn in the biggest foreign IPO in US history, with shares surging 17% on debut — the AI chip boom's largest Wall Street moment yet, and US officials are pressing for new American fabs. Automation angle: AI-infra capital flooding in means sustained downstream demand for people who make AI useful — your layer of the stack.

global_ai_news.title: Instagram’s Adam Mosseri: If you don’t like AI, ‘then you shouldn’t have it in your feed’
global_ai_news.summary: Instagram head Adam Mosseri says users who dislike AI content 'shouldn't have it in your feed' — but the platform won't filter it out by default, leaning on user controls instead. Automation angle: AI-content policy churn on big platforms is a recurring content topic with built-in search demand.

global_ai_news.title: Robot Dogs, Teslas, and Rescue Helicopters: The UN AI Summit Was a Lot
global_ai_news.summary: Wired's dispatch from the UN AI for Good summit: robot dogs and Silicon Valley optimism alongside an urgent question — can global governance catch up before the technology outruns it? Automation angle: governance debates are slow; builders who ship responsibly now set the de facto standards.

global_ai_news.title: Taiwanese chipmaker Nanya plans $6 billion in spending in 2027, riding AI boom
global_ai_news.summary: Taiwanese memory chipmaker Nanya plans $6 billion in 2027 capital spending, riding AI-driven memory demand — more evidence the AI buildout is repricing the entire memory supply chain. Automation angle: memory scarcity feeds inference costs; expect API prices to stay volatile into 2027.

indian_ai_industry.title: Kandou AI to open India chip design headquarters in Hyderabad
indian_ai_industry.summary: Kandou AI will open its India chip design headquarters in Hyderabad, adding a significant AI-silicon design footprint to the city's growing semiconductor cluster. Automation angle: AI chip design centres seed local tooling and ops roles — remote-friendly adjacent openings usually follow.

indian_ai_industry.title: India’s thumpN raises $3.75m for AI-powered live entertainment platform
indian_ai_industry.summary: Mumbai-based thumpN raised $3.75M for its AI-powered live entertainment platform, with backing from Paytm founder Vijay Shekhar Sharma's family office. It pitches itself as India's first AI-native live events company. Automation angle: freshly funded Indian AI startups hire fast — pitch automation help while teams are still small.

ai_self_improvement_rsi.title: Is recursive self‑improvement the dawning of AI superintelligence? - The Conversation
ai_self_improvement_rsi.summary: The Conversation examines whether recursive self-improvement — AI systems improving their own ability to improve — has actually begun, weighing today's AI-assisted AI research against true runaway feedback loops. Automation angle: the honest framing here is great teaching material for a video on what RSI is and isn't.

ai_self_improvement_rsi.title: I Built a Self-Improving AI, and So Can You
ai_self_improvement_rsi.summary: A Wired writer built a small self-improving AI system and argues the technique — AI writing and testing improvements to its own scaffolding — is now accessible to individual builders, not just frontier labs. Automation angle: replicate this as a portfolio project — a self-tuning agent loop is a standout demo for AI-automation roles.

ai_self_improvement_rsi.title: Is Recursive Self-Improvement Really Here? - Communications of the ACM
ai_self_improvement_rsi.summary: Communications of the ACM asks whether recursive self-improvement is really here, separating models that speed up AI research from systems that autonomously retrain themselves. Automation angle: cite this in interviews when asked about AI risk — it maps the capability frontier precisely.

ai_self_improvement_rsi.title: How Nvidia’s ASPIRE framework accelerates robot programming with self-improving AI - TechTalks
ai_self_improvement_rsi.summary: Nvidia's ASPIRE framework accelerates robot programming with self-improving AI: robots generate, test, and refine their own control code in simulation loops. Automation angle: self-improving codegen loops are coming to business automation next — the robotics pattern is the preview.

ai_self_improvement_rsi.title: Tool-Making and Self-Evolving LLM Agents in Low-Latency Systems
ai_self_improvement_rsi.summary: An arXiv paper describes production LLM agents that make their own tools: instead of regenerating code per request, agents promote repeated procedures into a persistent, self-evolving toolkit, cutting latency and failures. Automation angle: tool-making agents are directly buildable in Claude Code today — turn this paper into a working demo.

ai_self_improvement_rsi.title: Self-Improving AI Breaks Free From Big Tech's Grip - The Tech Buzz
ai_self_improvement_rsi.summary: The Tech Buzz reports on open self-improving AI projects moving the technique outside the big labs, with community systems that iteratively rewrite their own training and scaffolding code. Automation angle: open RSI tooling is a content goldmine — cover it before mainstream channels do.

ai_self_improvement_rsi.title: AI Stocks: Will Self-Improving Models Decide Who Wins Superintelligence Race? - Investor's Business Daily
ai_self_improvement_rsi.summary: Investor's Business Daily frames the AI stock race around self-improving models: labs whose systems can automate their own research may compound capability faster than rivals. Automation angle: markets pricing RSI as the winner-take-most variable tells you which employers will grow fastest.

ai_model_benchmarks.title: Text / LLM - Best: Claude Fable 5 / Runner-up: GPT-5.6 Sol
ai_model_benchmarks.summary: Current category leader: Claude Fable 5; runner-up GPT-5.6 Sol. Automation angle: switch your portfolio demos to whichever model ranks best in the category you build in this week.

ai_model_benchmarks.title: Coding - Best: Claude Fable 5 (via Claude Code) / Runner-up: Grok 4.5
ai_model_benchmarks.summary: Current category leader: Claude Fable 5 (via Claude Code); runner-up Grok 4.5. Automation angle: switch your portfolio demos to whichever model ranks best in the category you build in this week.

ai_model_benchmarks.title: Image Generation - Best: ChatGPT Images 2.0 / Runner-up: Meta Muse Image
ai_model_benchmarks.summary: Current category leader: ChatGPT Images 2.0; runner-up Meta Muse Image. Automation angle: switch your portfolio demos to whichever model ranks best in the category you build in this week.

ai_model_benchmarks.title: Video Generation - Best: Sora 2 / Runner-up: Google Veo 3
ai_model_benchmarks.summary: Current category leader: Sora 2; runner-up Google Veo 3. Automation angle: switch your portfolio demos to whichever model ranks best in the category you build in this week.

ai_model_benchmarks.title: Music / Audio - Best: Suno v5 / Runner-up: Udio v2.5
ai_model_benchmarks.summary: Current category leader: Suno v5; runner-up Udio v2.5. Automation angle: switch your portfolio demos to whichever model ranks best in the category you build in this week.

ai_model_benchmarks.title: Reasoning / Math - Best: Claude Fable 5 / Runner-up: GPT-5.6 Sol (xhigh)
ai_model_benchmarks.summary: Current category leader: Claude Fable 5; runner-up GPT-5.6 Sol (xhigh). Automation angle: switch your portfolio demos to whichever model ranks best in the category you build in this week.

ai_model_benchmarks.title: Ideas Have Genomes: Benchmarking Scientific Lineage Reasoning and Lineage-Grounded Idea Generation
ai_model_benchmarks.summary: This arXiv benchmark tests whether models can reason over the lineage of scientific ideas — which papers a concept inherits from — and generate ideas grounded in that lineage, a capability current benchmarks ignore. Automation angle: lineage-aware retrieval is a fresh angle for research-agent demos.

ai_model_benchmarks.title: AUTOPILOT VQA: Benchmarking Vision-Language Models for Incident-Centric Dashcam Understanding
ai_model_benchmarks.summary: AUTOPILOT VQA benchmarks vision-language models on incident-centric dashcam understanding for autonomous driving, probing whether VLMs can reason about accidents rather than just describe scenes. Automation angle: niche safety benchmarks like this reveal where VLM agents still fail — good gap material.

new_ai_tools.title: Quoting OpenAI
new_ai_tools.summary: Simon Willison highlights how OpenAI's ChatGPT Work runs agents in the cloud on web/mobile while the desktop app can execute locally — the same cloud/local split Claude Code users already navigate. Useful signal on where coding-agent execution models are converging. Automation angle: study the execution-environment split — it's the key design decision in every agent product you'll build or review.

new_ai_tools.title: OpenAI staffer maps out which of GPT-5.6 Sol's five reasoning levels fits which task complexity
new_ai_tools.summary: An OpenAI staffer mapped which of GPT-5.6 Sol's five reasoning levels (Light through xhigh, plus multi-agent Max/Ultra modes) fits which task complexity, recommending starting low and escalating. Practical guidance for anyone routing work across model tiers. Automation angle: tiered-reasoning routing is directly portable to Claude Code effort levels — encode it into your workflows for instant cost wins.

new_ai_tools.title: OpenAI kills its Atlas browser after just eight months and folds everything into ChatGPT
new_ai_tools.summary: OpenAI killed its Atlas browser after eight months and folded agentic browsing into the ChatGPT desktop app and a Chrome extension. Standalone AI browsers keep dying; browsing is becoming a feature inside agents instead. Automation angle: don't build on standalone AI browsers — target extensions and agent-native browsing APIs that survive product resets.

new_ai_tools.title: OpenAI says GPT 5.6 is the ‘preferred model’ for Microsoft Copilot 365 amid breakup chatter
new_ai_tools.summary: OpenAI says GPT-5.6 is now the preferred model across Microsoft 365 Copilot — Word, Excel, PowerPoint, and Chat — amid continuing breakup chatter between the two companies. Enterprise office automation just got a model upgrade by default. Automation angle: Copilot upgrades reset what 'baseline automation' means for clients — sell what Copilot still can't do: cross-app agent workflows.

new_ai_tools.title: OpenAI Launches GPT-5.6, ChatGPT Work To Take On Claude Cowork
new_ai_tools.summary: OpenAI launched GPT-5.6 alongside ChatGPT Work, an agentic workspace that executes tasks across applications and files — a direct answer to Claude Cowork. Automation angle: run the same task through ChatGPT Work and Claude Cowork and publish the comparison — instant, timely portfolio content.

general_news.title: Wildfire in southern Spain leaves at least 12 dead and 23 missing
general_news.summary: A wildfire near Los Gallardos in Almería, southern Spain, has killed at least 12 people with 23 missing; officials say four victims may be British.

general_news.title: East Asia braces for destructive typhoon as landslides kill 15 in Philippines
general_news.summary: East Asia is bracing for Typhoon Bavi, a 1,000 km-wide storm forecast to be among the strongest in decades as it heads for Taiwan and southeastern China, after landslides killed 15 in the Philippines.

general_news.title: 'Agreed To Continue Talks, But Ceasefire Over': Trump's Mixed Iran Message
general_news.summary: Donald Trump delivered a mixed message on Iran, saying the ceasefire is over while agreeing to continue peace talks at Tehran's request — deepening uncertainty in West Asia days after Ayatollah Khamenei's funeral.

general_news.title: China lands reusable rocket for first time, state media says
general_news.summary: China landed a reusable rocket booster for the first time, on an offshore platform — state media hailed a historic breakthrough that narrows the gap with SpaceX and Blue Origin.

general_news.title: NATO Wants Members To Spend 5% Of GDP On Defence, Only Two Already Do
general_news.summary: NATO wants all members to spend 5% of GDP on defence, but only Lithuania and Estonia — both on the alliance's eastern flank — currently meet the target.

general_news.title: One Million Women Lose Access To Vital Support Amid Aid Cuts: UN
general_news.summary: The UN says one million women have lost access to vital support services amid global aid cuts, with 65% of women-led organisations reporting staff working without pay.

general_news.title: Man nearly sucked out of window mid-air on Ryanair plane, passengers say
general_news.summary: Passengers say a man was nearly sucked out of a window mid-flight on a Ryanair plane; his wife held his legs for around five minutes until the aircraft made an emergency descent.
